message:
beads: open wide-string harness-matrix coverage tasks

Adds two new conformance-harness gap beads:

- bd-jtix: wide_string_ops.json currently only executes through the
  in-process frankenlibc_fixture_exec path. The isolated harness
  subprocess (matrix) path is uncovered, leaving any
  subprocess-specific divergence invisible. The bead tracks extending
  the wide_string_ops_conformance_test to assert both expected_output
  and host parity per mode via the matrix path and fixing any
  subprocess-only regressions surfaced.

- bd-x5c9: wide_string.json has the same structural gap — the
  conformance test validates fixture metadata and executes in-process
  but never runs the matrix subprocess, so parity work in strict /
  hardened modes is untested end-to-end. Bead tracks adding harness
  matrix execution alongside the existing assertions.

Both are P2 tasks, open, no dependencies declared.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-j946","title":"vDSO gettime fastpath: add parity + fallback coverage","description":"Review of cod's recent vDSO fastpath (commit 92989d2/time_abi.rs) found coverage gaps in tests: (1) vdso_fastpath_snapshot and hit-counter tests confirm the fastpath is HIT but never verify the fastpath AGREES with the syscall fallback — a silent divergence (e.g., wrong clock_id mapping) would pass the existing tests. (2) No test verifies that CLOCK_PROCESS_CPUTIME_ID (not vDSO-exported) does NOT increment vdso_clock_gettime_hits. (3) No test for EINVAL propagation when vdso_clock_gettime rejects an unsupported clock. Scope: crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/time_abi_test.rs. Use raw_syscall::sys_clock_gettime as ground-truth reference to avoid libc-symbol interception issues.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-20T19:05:29.950988891Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-20T19:08:43.790037921Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T19:08:43.787823869Z","close_reason":"Added 2 vDSO parity tests in commit cbba2a6 (CLOCK_REALTIME and CLOCK_MONOTONIC vDSO vs raw-syscall agreement). Dropped the 2 cputime counter tests planned — global hit counter can't be asserted under Cargo test parallelism. Filed bd-1isd for minor defensive-handling issues spotted during the review (positive rc errno bug, per-arch LINUX_2.6 version string).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Add isolated harness subprocess coverage for tests/conformance/fixtures/wide_string_ops.json, assert expected_output and host parity per mode, and fix any divergence exposed by the matrix path.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:12:06.305846872Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:12:06.305846872Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "bd-ju7", "title": "Drift: runtime_math module cadence classification (hot path vs cadence; strict vs hardened)", "description": "Create an explicit classification of every runtime_math module (existing + new) along these axes:

- Hot-path vs cadence:
  - `hot`: runs inside `RuntimeMathKernel::decide` strict fast path
  - `cadence`: runs only on epoch boundaries / resample points
- Mode surface:
  - used in strict
  - used only in hardened
- Data dependencies:
  - uses only integer/fixed-point state
  - uses floats (must be cadence-only if strict budget matters)
  - uses locks/mutex (must not be on strict hot path)

## Why This Matters
This is the “extreme optimization” guardrail: we can’t reason about perf without knowing which modules execute when.

## Deliverable
- A table (in a bead comment or short doc) listing each module and its classification.
- The table must include legacy anchors: which API families/subsystems the module protects.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No module remains “unknown”; every module is classified.
- Any module marked `hot` has an explicit cost budget and no forbidden operations.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "DustyPuma", "created_at": "2026-02-09T22:03:30.665023416Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-10T07:18:36.592931590Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-10T07:18:36.592907995Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-ju7", "depends_on_id": "bd-1tx", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-ju7", "depends_on_id": "bd-1wy", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1101, "issue_id": "bd-ju7", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "Completed by DustyPuma. Full cadence classification of all 45 runtime_math modules.

## Architecture Pattern: Deferred-Update

```
decide() [hot path, every call]
├─ AtomicU8/U64 reads (43 modules) — lock-free, integer only
├─ Direct method calls (2 modules: risk, cohomology) — lock-free
└─ Every 64th call: resample_high_order_kernels()
    └─ risk.evaluate() [lock-free], oracle.lock() [Mutex]

observe_validation_result() [cold path, after validation]
├─ 43 Mutex-protected modules updated sequentially
│  └─ Each state encoded to AtomicU8 and stored
└─ 25-element severity vector fed to fusion
   └─ 18 meta-controllers updated in second cascade
```

## Hot-Path Modules (2, both lock-free):
1. **risk** — `upper_bound_ppm()` direct call, conformal arithmetic, u32
2. **cohomology** — `fault_count()` direct call, u64 counter

## Cadence-Only Modules (43, behind Mutex in observe):
All follow the pattern: Mutex<Controller> updated in observe → state encoded to AtomicU8 → read lock-free in decide.

### AtomicU8 scalar (37 modules):
spectral, rough_path, persistence, bridge, hji, mfg, padic, symplectic, sparse, topos, audit, changepoint, conformal, loss_minimizer, coupling, microlocal, serre, clifford, ktheory, covering, tstructure, atiyah_bott, pomdp, sos, admm, obstruction, operator_norm, malliavin, info_geometry, matrix_concentration, nerve_complex, wasserstein, kernel_mmd, pac_bayes, stein, lyapunov, rademacher, transfer_entropy, hodge, provenance, grobner, grothendieck

### AtomicU8[COUNT] family-indexed (3 modules):
eprocess, cvar, large_deviations

### AtomicU64 multi-field (3 modules):
design (ident_ppm, budget_ns, expected_ns, selected), fusion (bonus_ppm, entropy_milli, drift_ppm, dominant_signal), equivariant (alignment_ppm, orbit)

## Mode Gating: NONE
All 45 modules run in BOTH strict and hardened modes. Mode only affects action selection (Allow vs Repair), not controller activation.

## Barrier Oracle:
Called lock-free in decide() at line 1253 — `barrier.admissible()`. Read-only guard, no observation needed. Classified as **hot/read-only**.

## Design Subsystem:
Special case — Mutex acquired in decide() but ONLY every 256th call (line ~1157). Cadence-gated.

## Summary:
- 0 modules with forbidden ops on strict hot path (no exp/ln/matrix)
- All float computation deferred to observe phase behind Mutex
- decide() hot path is 100% integer/atomic reads + 2 lock-free calls
- Strict overhead dominated by bonus summation (~40 atomic loads)", "created_at": "2026-02-10T07:18:31Z"}]}

Deliverables:
- Define default production kernel (small, explainable, benchmarked).
- Add compile-time feature gates separating production vs research modules.

Acceptance:
- Default build excludes ResearchOnly modules.
- Production kernel meets strict/hardened latency budgets.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.", "notes": "Perf gate rerun on idle host passed (scripts/perf_gate.sh): strict deltas within threshold, hardened decide_observe delta +1.74%. However target budget checks still rely on allow_target_violation policy in perf gate, and manifest currently keeps all runtime_math modules in production with research_only_modules empty. Bead remains in_progress pending minimal production subset selection + explicit research-only migration plan.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "AzureBluff", "created_at": "2026-02-11T02:48:11.222573367Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-19T16:39:50.848168045Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-19T16:39:50.848140263Z", "close_reason": "Completed minimal production kernel manifest partition (25 production / 44 research-only), synchronized governance/policy artifacts, and validated via runtime-math gates + rch harness tests.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["alien", "critique", "math"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-rqn", "depends_on_id": "bd-1iya", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-rqn", "depends_on_id": "bd-1rxj", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-rqn", "depends_on_id": "bd-24x", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-rqn", "depends_on_id": "bd-25pf", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-rqn", "depends_on_id": "bd-2k6b", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-rqn", "depends_on_id": "bd-7dw2", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:33Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1136, "issue_id": "bd-rqn", "author": "Codex", "text": "Codex here. Ledger now includes in_production_manifest and in_research_only_manifest booleans. Validation: python3 scripts/runtime_math_admission_gate.py -> pass; rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_admission_gate_test -> PASS (3/3).", "created_at": "2026-02-15T17:41:49Z"}, {"id": 1141, "issue_id": "bd-rqn", "author": "AzureBluff", "text": "Progress update (AzureBluff, 2026-02-19): completed production-kernel partition and governance artifact alignment to unblock bd-3ot.1.

Implemented:
- tests/runtime_math/production_kernel_manifest.v1.json: moved to minimal production set (25 modules) and explicit research_only_modules (44 modules).
- tests/conformance/math_production_set_policy.v1.json: updated change_gate.manifest_sha256 to b47cd1f6283dc3bb151fb0c57b7f8ef5c1b1afe526cf2b333d92eecc37ef257b, module_count=25, and summary counts to production-only.
- tests/conformance/math_retirement_policy.json: reflected completed migration state (RC-1=0, no active waivers, migration waves=0, summary updated).
- tests/runtime_math/runtime_math_classification_matrix.v1.json: synchronized per-module in_production_manifest / in_research_only_manifest flags and summary counts with new manifest partition.
- Re-ran scripts/runtime_math_admission_gate.py to regenerate:
  - tests/runtime_math/admission_gate_report.v1.json
  - tests/runtime_math/controller_manifest.v1.json

Gate/script verification (PASS):
- scripts/check_runtime_math_manifest.sh
- scripts/check_runtime_math_classification_matrix.sh
- scripts/check_math_production_set_policy.sh
- scripts/check_math_retirement.sh
- scripts/check_runtime_math_admission.sh
- scripts/check_math_governance.sh

RCH validation:
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_admission_gate_test (PASS 4/4)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test runtime_math_classification_matrix_test (PASS 8/8)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test math_production_set_policy_test (PASS 4/4)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test math_retirement_test (PASS 9/9)
- rch exec -- cargo fmt --check (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets started remotely and progressed through broad suite but stalled in long pthread lifecycle tests; process was terminated to avoid indefinite hang.

This resolves bd-rqn’s outstanding blocker note: production manifest is now minimal, research-only routing is explicit, and policy artifacts are auditable + reproducible.

 {"id": "bd-rth1", "title": "bd-yos subtask: TLS handoff and teardown correctness for spawned threads", "description": "Background:
- TLS handoff correctness is required for thread-local errno and future pthread_key destructor behavior.

Goal:
- Implement and verify TLS initialization/teardown handoff for spawned threads.

Deliverables:
1) TLS init protocol during thread start.
2) Per-thread errno/TLS storage linkage checks.
3) Teardown sequencing and cleanup invariants.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Thread-local values remain isolated and stable under concurrent workloads.
- TLS teardown leaves no leaked state.

Verification & Logging:
- Unit tests for TLS setup/teardown and isolation.
- E2E concurrent tests validating cross-thread isolation.
- Structured logs with trace_id, thread_id, tls_event, key_count, cleanup_status.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "Codex", "created_at": "2026-02-12T15:00:52.128037997Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-22T06:12:33.353261867Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-22T06:12:33.353242521Z", "close_reason": "Completed TLS handoff/teardown correctness fixes and verification evidence posted in comments.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["critique", "implementation", "pthread", "testing", "tls"], "comments": [{"id": 1142, "issue_id": "bd-rth1", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "Codex claiming this now. Found a concrete TLS table correctness bug: removing one TID currently breaks linear-probe lookup chains for colliding TIDs, which can corrupt per-thread TLS lookup after teardown. I am implementing tombstone-based delete + collision tests now.", "created_at": "2026-02-22T05:19:32Z"}, {"id": 1143, "issue_id": "bd-rth1", "author": "Codex", "text": "Completed TLS handoff/teardown hardening and verification.

 {"id": "bd-s39o", "title": "EPIC: Expand LD_PRELOAD program compatibility beyond 61/61 core suite", "description": "FrankenLibC currently passes 61/61 programs in the core LD_PRELOAD test suite PLUS 12+ complex programs (Python, Perl, Node.js, SSH, OpenSSL, Bash, Zsh, Vim, less, nano, curl, make, tar, gzip).

GOAL: Systematically expand compatibility to cover more real-world workloads. The next targets should be programs that exercise features we haven't fully tested:
- Database clients (psql, mysql, sqlite3)
- Build tools (cmake, ninja, gcc, rustc)
- Network tools (wget, dig, nslookup)
- Compression (bzip2, xz, zstd)
- Package managers (apt, dpkg)
- Editors/pagers (emacs, man)

APPROACH: Test each program, capture failure backtraces, fix root causes. Each fix typically benefits many programs.

ALREADY WORKING (verified):
61 core: cat ls echo wc head tail sort grep sed awk date env id uname whoami hostname pwd basename dirname mkdir rmdir touch rm cp mv ln chmod stat file dd tr cut paste fold uniq comm tee xargs yes seq expr test true false printf readlink realpath nproc getconf stty tty logname uptime arch git df du free kill sleep nice
Complex: python3 perl node curl make tar gzip ssh openssl bash zsh vim less nano", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "feature", "assignee": "PlumCove", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:05:51.740882739Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-02T19:47:50.422019583Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-02T19:47:50.421726608Z", "close_reason": "All children closed: bd-s39o.1 (database clients), bd-s39o.2 (build tools), bd-s39o.3 (network tools). 61/61 core suite + 12+ complex programs pass.  {"id": "bd-wuh", "title": "EPIC: RaptorQ-inspired runtime resilience (erasure-coded evidence + metadata self-healing)", "description": "Goal: use RaptorQ patterns accretively inside libc runtime, without adding /dp/asupersync as a runtime dependency.

Core idea (pattern, not copy-paste):
- Systematic fast path: store source symbols (raw evidence) cheaply.
- Repair path: generate deterministic repair symbols on a cadence (epoch boundary), not per call.
- Decode + proof: decoding and explainability happen in diagnostics/harness, not on the strict hot path.

Candidate uses:
1) Erasure-coded evidence ring buffer for runtime_math decisions/heals/anomalies.
2) Self-healing for small critical metadata capsules (headers/roots), not entire large tables.
3) Cross-shard summary reconstruction to support cohomology/consistency monitors under partial loss.

Success criteria:
- Strict fast path unchanged; hardened adds bounded overhead only when enabled.
- Deterministic encoding parameters + evidence-ledger tuning of redundancy.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "feature", "created_at": "2026-02-09T21:30:15.573551832Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-11T03:03:13.254498204Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-11T03:03:13.145127019Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-wuh", "depends_on_id": "bd-284", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-wuh", "depends_on_id": "bd-2xr", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-wuh", "depends_on_id": "bd-3a9", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-wuh", "depends_on_id": "bd-3ku", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-wuh", "depends_on_id": "bd-pc4", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-02-26T06:17:34Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}], "comments": [{"id": 1215, "issue_id": "bd-wuh", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "## Epic Notes: RaptorQ-Inspired Resilience Inside libc Runtime

### North Star
Turn fragile “best effort” telemetry into **recoverable, explainable evidence** under partial loss, while keeping libc hot paths fast.

RaptorQ is the reference pattern because it gives us:
- systematic fast path (zero overhead when nothing is lost)
- deterministic repair generation (no coordination required)
- recovery from erasures (dropped/missing records) with small overhead
- explainability via decode proofs (in tooling)

### Non-Negotiable Constraints
- No `/dp/asupersync` in the runtime dependency graph.
- Strict mode hot path stays “boring”: constant-time, no allocations, no locks.
- Hardened mode overhead is bounded and measured (microbench + regression gates).

### Concrete libc Use Cases (Scope)
1. **Erasure-coded evidence ring**
   - Evidence includes: runtime_math decision + key context + membrane outcome + heal action + stage exits.
   - Goal: if we drop N% of events or overwrite parts of the ring, tooling can still reconstruct a consistent prefix / windows.
2. **Self-healing critical metadata capsules**
   - Small, high-value state snapshots (kernel snapshot schema, policy tables hash, config epoch) should survive partial corruption.
3. **Cross-shard reconstruction for consistency monitors**
   - Cohomology/consistency monitors can treat “missing overlap witnesses” as erasures and reconstruct summaries.

### Runtime Design Principles (Extreme Optimization)
- Evidence recording per call:
  - `seq = atomic_fetch_add(Relaxed)`
  - store a fixed-size record into a per-thread ring slot
  - no heap alloc
  - no syscalls
  - no GF(256) operations
- Repair symbol generation:
  - done on cadence: every `K_source` events, per-thread or global epoch
  - uses deterministic seed; work amortized
  - may run in a background worker thread only in hardened mode
- Export:
  - tooling/harness reads the ring (or a frozen snapshot) and performs decode + proof.

### Reliability Model
We model losses as:
- erasures: records not present (ring overwrite, sampling drop, partial capture)
- corruption: payload hash mismatch (bit flips, races)

We treat corruption as “erase and try to repair” (don’t let bad bytes poison decode).

### Adaptive Redundancy (Control)
- Observe: loss rate (erasures) and corruption rate in exported traces.
- Decide: raise redundancy when anytime-valid evidence says we’re violating durability/observability budgets.
- Act: increase `R` (repair symbols) in future epochs; optionally retro-harden recent epochs.

This is implemented via `bd-284` and wired to runtime_math e-process + evidence ledger.

### Deliverables / Done Means
- Encoding path exists with strict-mode overhead indistinguishable from baseline.
- Offline decode exists and verifies record integrity + hash-chain.
- Loss/corruption simulation tests demonstrate reconstruction up to documented budgets.
- Evidence reports are explainable: when repair happens, tooling emits a DecodeProof.", "created_at": "2026-02-09T21:50:51Z"}, {"id": 1216, "issue_id": "bd-wuh", "author": "Dicklesworthstone", "text": "EPIC COMPLETE. All 5 blockers resolved: bd-3a9 (architecture), bd-pc4 (offline decoder), bd-2xr (loss simulation), bd-3ku (evidence integration), bd-284 (adaptive tuner). Evidence ring buffer integrated, adaptive redundancy tuner with e-process + audit ledger, 69 modules, all quality gates passing.", "created_at": "2026-02-11T03:03:13Z"}]}

- E2E scripts with comprehensive structured logs and artifact refs.", "acceptance_criteria": "## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.", "notes": "2026-02-18 status (IcyCat): NSS fixture executor wiring completed in crates/frankenlibc_conformance/src/lib.rs; NSS fixture packs expanded (pwd_ops/grp_ops/resolver); new integration fixture tests/integration/fixture_nss.c added and c_fixture_spec/link_test updated. Verification: cargo fmt --check PASS; rch cargo check/clippy PASS; rch cargo test -p frankenlibc_conformance PASS (57 tests); rch bash scripts/check_c_fixture_suite.sh PASS. Implemented in `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_thread_lifecycle_test.rs`:
- Added deterministic stress harness helpers (`env_usize`, `run_create_join_roundtrip_iters`, `run_detach_join_esrch_iters`).
- Converted lifecycle stress tests to bounded defaults to prevent CI hangs:
  - `pthread_create_join_roundtrip_stress` now uses `FRANKENLIBC_THREAD_ROUNDTRIP_STRESS_ITERS` (default `16`, cap `128`).
  - `pthread_create_join_parallel_batch_stress` now uses `FRANKENLIBC_THREAD_PARALLEL_BATCH` (default `8`, cap `32`).
  - `pthread_detach_join_esrch_stress` now uses `FRANKENLIBC_THREAD_DETACH_STRESS_ITERS` (default `16`, cap `128`).
- Added explicit opt-in endurance tests (ignored by default):
  - `pthread_create_join_roundtrip_long_stress_profile`
  - `pthread_detach_join_esrch_long_stress_profile`

Validation run (CPU-intensive cargo via `rch`):
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-dustyriver cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_thread_lifecycle_test -- --nocapture` ✅ (`8 passed, 2 ignored`)
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-dustyriver cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test pthread_cond_core_test -- --nocapture` ✅ (`6 passed`)
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-dustyriver cargo fmt --check` ✅
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-dustyriver cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅
- `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-dustyriver cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅

Notes:
- `rch` remote daemon intermittently timed out in this environment; fail-open local execution was used where needed (per AGENTS.md behavior).
- `bd-xxd9` remains `in_progress`; next step is extending regression signatures toward known LD_PRELOAD deadlock/timeout patterns while preserving deterministic runtimes.

  - `pthread_create_join_parallel_batch_stress`
  - `pthread_detach_join_esrch_stress`
- Long-run stress variants remain ignored and available for explicit endurance runs.

  - Repro came from a self-join regression attempt that could wedge the lifecycle suite.
